titles.en: Reward of reciting a hundred verses of the Holy Quran and saying Yaa Allaah seven times
Reward of reciting a hundred verses of the Holy Quran and saying Yaa Allaah seven times

segments: : مَنْ قَرَأَ مِائَهَ آیَهٍ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ أَیِّ الْقُرْآنِ شَاءَ ثُمَّ قَالَ یَا اللَّهُ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ فَلَوْ دَعَا عَلَی الصَّخْرَهِ لَقَلَعَهَا إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.
arabic.text: : مَنْ قَرَأَ مِائَهَ آیَهٍ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ أَیِّ الْقُرْآنِ شَاءَ ثُمَّ قَالَ یَا اللَّهُ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ فَلَوْ دَعَا عَلَی الصَّخْرَهِ لَقَلَعَهَا إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ.

segments: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Ali b. Asbat who raised it to Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) who said: “One who says Yaa Allaah seven times after reciting a hundred verses of the Holy Quran from anywhere, even if he prays against Sakhr (a stone) it would be dislodged from its place.”
en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs.text: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh from his uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from Ali b. Asbat who raised it to Ameer al-Mumineen (a.s.) who said: “One who says Yaa Allaah seven times after reciting a hundred verses of the Holy Quran from anywhere, even if he prays against Sakhr (a stone) it would be dislodged from its place.”

narratorChain.raw: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ یَرْفَعُهُ إِلَی أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع قَالَ

parts.text: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ
عَلِیِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ یَرْفَعُهُ إِلَی أَمِیرِ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع
